h1: Calculate Your 15% Cedular Investment Income Tax
description: Calculate your 15% cedular tax on investment income. Fast, accurate calculator for stocks, bonds, crypto & dividends. Updated 2026.
intro: Personal finance operates on universal principles: compound interest, diversification, long-term horizons, and low costs. These tools work across global markets. The 15% cedular tax applies to financial investment income in Argentina. Our calculator is updated for 2026 and reviewed regularly to keep you current.
keyTakeaway: Formula: **investment gain × 15%**. Direct application with your inputs. The 15% cedular tax rate applies to all financial investment income.
useCases: Technical calculations for cedular financial income tax planning
Learn and teach cedular investment tax concepts
Quick reference for tax planning projects
explanation:
Personal finance and investing apply universal mathematical principles (compound interest, time value of money, risk-return optimization). This calculator applies standard financial industry formulas.

## How It's Calculated

The 15% cedular tax applies to financial investment income in Argentina.

This calculator applies the formula using your inputs, checking typical ranges and flagging values outside reasonable expectations.

## Typical Returns by Time Horizon

| Time Horizon | S&P 500 | 10-Year Bonds | Cash |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 year | Highly variable | 4-5% | 3-5% |
| 5 years | ~8% avg | ~4% | Inflation |
| 10 years | ~9-10% | ~4% | <inflation |
| 20+ years | ~10% nominal | ~5% | N/A |

## Investment Principles

- **Stick to your plan** during market downturns. Emotion is the worst advisor.
- **Diversify**: Don't concentrate in a single asset, sector, or country.
- **Keep costs low**: Fees over 1% annually eat 30% of returns over 30 years.
- **Long time horizon**: Equities work over 10+ years, not 1 year.
- **Rebalance once yearly**: Not more frequently.

## Final Notes

This calculation is an informational reference only. For critical decisions (financial, medical, legal), consult with a licensed professional. Values are current for 2026 and reviewed periodically.

faq.q: Who does the 15% cedular tax apply to?
faq.a: Individuals and undivided estates on dividends, interest, and capital gains.
Yes, peso-denominated fixed-term deposits are exempt under Argentine law 27,430.
Yes, CEDEAR dividends are subject to the 15% cedular tax.
Cryptocurrency gains are taxed at 15% on realized gains in USD.

faq.q: Can I offset losses against gains?
faq.a: Yes, losses and gains within the cedular category can be offset.

faq.q: When do I report cedular income?
faq.a: Annual reporting on personal income tax returns (DDJJ Ganancias).

faq.q: What returns should I expect from the market?
faq.a: S&P 500 historical average: ~10% nominal annually (7% inflation-adjusted). Past performance doesn't predict future returns but provides context.
Dividends, interest, capital gains on stocks, bonds, funds, crypto, and other financial assets subject to the cedular tax.

sources.name: NIST — National Institute of Standards and Technology

dataUpdate.notes: Fixed formula, does not require external data updates
